Please support customers by highlighting these issues in your videos. Many of us watch your content and make purchase decisions based on it. But what often happens is disappointing. Once a new variant is launched, the older model seems to get downgraded through software updates.
After the January 2026 update, the zoom feature on my X200 FE completely stopped working. When I visited the service center, they told me the camera hardware needs to be replaced. If I go ahead with that, I will lose the phone’s IP rating.
We paid ₹50,000 for this device. Now there’s no proper camera performance and the overall software experience has also degraded. This is not what customers expect after investing that much money.

Could you please highlight the issue so that companies like Vivo focus on genuine service.
Translated:
Please support customers by highlighting these issues in your videos. Many of us watch your content and make purchase decisions based on it. But what often happens is disappointing. Once a new variant is launched, the older model seems to get downgraded through software updates.
After the January 2026 update, the zoom feature on my X200 FE completely stopped working. When I visited the service center, they told me the camera hardware needs to be replaced. If I go ahead with that, I will lose the phone’s IP rating.

Could you please highlight the issue so that companies like Vivo focus on genuine service.

If I open YouTube and watch the video, it seems that everything has failed in front of Vivo x200 Fe and when I look at the comments, it seems that not buying Vivo v60 would be the biggest mistake of life.

Text:
When considering a smartphone with a curved or quad-curved display, it's important to weigh the benefits against the drawbacks. While these designs may look sleek and elegant, they come with several practical disadvantages that can affect your long-term user experience.

One major issue is the cost of repairs. Curved displays are more expensive to replace due to their complexity and the delicate nature of the curved glass. This makes the device harder and pricier to fix compared to standard flat screens.

Additionally, the curvature of the screen can cause text and images to appear distorted along the edges, making reading and viewing less comfortable. This distortion becomes more noticeable over time, which can diminish the overall enjoyment of using your phone.

Another problem is the lack of compatible accessories. Tempered glass screen protectors, for example, are hard to find for curved screens, and the ones that are available—often UV glue-based—can damage the phone's display if not applied properly.

Ultimately, while curved displays may offer a stylish design, the practicality of a flat display is hard to beat. The decision to move away from curved screens, as seen with Samsung phasing them out and Apple never adopting them in the first place, suggests that tech giants prioritize usability and durability over aesthetics.

So, before opting for a curved or quad-curved smartphone, it’s worth considering whether the flashy look is worth the trade-offs in terms of practicality, repair costs, and long-term functionality.

Text:
I have been using the same device from last two months and here is my review for this phone.

After using the device for two months, I've encountered numerous bugs that significantly detract from the overall experience. While the camera quality, display, color accuracy, and battery life are commendable, the software performance leaves much to be desired. The Amazon app frequently fails to load thumbnails, and during Netflix streaming, the video often freezes while the audio continues uninterrupted. Additionally, I've experienced frequent call drops, and enabling the Wi-Fi calling feature results in audio jittering. To make matters worse, the phone tends to overheat even during light usage, not just gaming or intensive tasks. It's clear that while reviewers often highlight the positives due to brand influence, honest feedback is crucial for potential buyers to make informed decisions.

I hope this helps.
Thanks!
Translated:
I have been using the same device from last two months and here is my review for this phone.

After using the device for two months, I've encountered numerous bugs that significantly detract from the overall experience. While the camera quality, display, color accuracy, and battery life are commendable, the software performance leaves much to be desired. The Amazon app frequently fails to load thumbnails, and during Netflix streaming, the video often freezes while the audio continues uninterrupted. Additionally, I've experienced frequent call drops, and enabling the Wi-Fi calling feature results in audio jittering. To make matters worse, the phone tends to overheat even during light usage, not just gaming or intensive tasks. It's clear that while reviewers often highlight the positives due to brand influence, honest feedback is crucial for potential buyers to make informed decisions.

I hope this helps.
Thanks!

Text:
No one likes to see this especially if you are not a heavy/power user but these are the things I wished had came up before I decided to buy the vivo phone.

Comparing to the Samsung one UI, the software is severely behind. Feels like I'm using a samsung galaxy s3 software.

1. Notes app 
- can't do sub bullets

2. Gallery app 
- can't view in list mode
- gallery doesn't stay in 8x8 after resizing from 4x4.
- text in gallery is non customizable. Font is too big.


3. Camera
- Camera flickers a lot on the vivo than the samsung in low light l.e.d condition. (think aquariums, museums). Picture and videos taken have the flicker banding on it
- can't handle brightness well i.e subject infront of a bright light
- Auto focus is haywire. The phone seems very confused where/what to focus on.
- Videos shot on pro mode are not compatible to play on my laptop even with VLC player despite the settings being at h.264, 30 fps, 1080p. However, the videos that are using the same settings on normal video mode are playable on my laptop.
- zeiss and texture dulls the orange yet vivid enhances
- camera rattles, which people will claim that it is normal due to the Optical Image Stabilization (OIS) system. But even as I'm recording, no matter what modes I'm using, the rattling sound gets recorded as well as I shake the camera.

4. Display
- Screen color on natural and professional, desaturates the orange. While on bright profile, orange is too saturated. 

5. Buggy phone?
- Settings disappearing like global search. Other users on the same phone declares that they have the global search but yet my x300 pro doesn't have it. They say you can swipe down gestures or activate it in settings. But my settings have nothing except "Notification Centre and Control Panel".
- Despite turning off camera shutter sound, the sound stills plays on certain apps. It might not be a vivo bug but the fault of the app publisher. But this also shows that because vivo is not a known brand, apps are less likely to optimize for vivo phones.
- When click 'open in app' after opening links from youtube/instagram/etc that directed me to a webpage, it doesn't open the app and instead bring me back to the webpage with 'Open in app'. The apps that I've installed doesn't appear in the list of apps in 'Open with..'

6. Unintuitive 
- Search function in Settings doesn't make sense. I.e If I search 'Display', 'Display, brightness and eye protection' should be the first thing that comes up and not "Battery percentage display mode"- which got to do with the battery more than the display.
- anything that requires editing Time (clock app, settings app) doesn't allow users to tap and type the numbers, users are forced to spin the scrolling wheel to the desired number.
- Brightness slider can only slide instead of tap & slide.
- No settings for quick access to notification panel tools like brightness, torch light, blue tooth, while phone is locked.
- swipe to unlock phone seems to have a limited area of touch where if you somehow didn't get the correct angle, the phone would not budge. 
- gboard needs you to tap and hold space bar to change a language. While finding other keyboard should be able to solve the problems, other keyboards come with another set of problems. I.e Microsoft Swiftkey swipe keyboard - lack of certain language types, no way quick way of switching language, has more steps than gboard.
- Cannot see time remaining left to full charge
- No settings to automatically remove status bar from screenshots. Sure you can crop it, but cropping it means -> Press on screenshot taken> Drag the crop to the desired outcome which may not be consistent with all the previous screenshot> Press the Save button on the top right corner > Wait for it to process > Send the picture. As opposed to Take a screenshot > Send the picture.
- Notifications doesn't preview the entire passage of text. Will cut off at a certain point. Whereas on Samsung I could preview 3 paragraphs of text from the notification panel.

Text:
"गुरुजी नमस्कार, मैंने ₹15,000 में 4GB RAM वाला मोबाइल फोन खरीदा था, लेकिन अपडेट के बाद उसमें सिर्फ 813 MB ही usable RAM मिल रही है। यही हाल मेरे 8GB RAM वाले दूसरे मोबाइल में भी हो रहा है — ज़्यादातर RAM सिस्टम ही खा रहा है।

तो क्या कंपनियाँ वर्चुअल RAM के नाम पर लोगों को बेवकूफ बना रही हैं? कृपया इस विषय पर एक बार वीडियो ज़रूर बनाएं और कंपनियों तक भी यह बात पहुँचाएं कि ग्राहकों को कम से कम 3GB usable RAM तो मिलनी ही चाहिए।

साथ ही, कृपया यह भी बताएं कि लॉन्ग टर्म के लिए ऐसा कौन-सा मोबाइल लेना चाहिए जो अपडेट के बाद स्लो या हैंग न हो।”
Translated:
"Guruji Namaskar, I had bought a mobile phone with 4GB RAM for ₹15,000, but after the update, it is getting only 813 MB of usable RAM. The same is happening with my other mobile with 8GB RAM - most of the RAM is being consumed by the system.

So are companies fooling people in the name of Virtual RAM? Please make a video on this topic once and convey this to the companies that customers should get at least 3GB of usable RAM.

Also, please also tell which mobile should be taken for long term which does not slow down or hang after the update.”
